rows.Risk Transfer Flow: Investors → MultiCat SPV → retrocession to Swiss Re → reinsurance via Agroasemex → FONDEN
rows.Notes on Structure: Three parametric triggers (one per class); Class C supports 50% or 100% payments based on central pressure.

rows.Risk Transfer Flow: Investors → IBRD notes → retrocession chain via Swiss Re (as described) → protection to FONDEN
rows.Notes on Structure: Two series (118 & 119) sold to ILS investors; IBRD enters risk-transfer arrangement ultimately benefiting FONDEN.

rows.Risk Transfer Flow: Investors → IBRD → retrocessional reinsurance agreements (4 tranches) → disaster insurance to Mexico (FONDEN trustees structure described)
rows.Additional Risk Transfer Instruments: Sustainable development bond feature (proceeds fund development projects)
rows.Notes on Structure: Multi-tranche quake & named storm protection; overlapping US areas included in covered zone description.

rows.Risk Transfer Flow: Investors → IBRD CAR notes → Munich Re retrocession front → Agroasemex → Mexico Treasury
rows.Notes on Structure: Three tranches (2 EQ, 1 Atlantic) with parametric payout tiers including linear named storm factor.

rows.Risk Transfer Flow: Investors → IBRD CAR notes → Munich Re retrocession front → Agroasemex → Mexico Treasury
rows.Notes on Structure: Single tranche; investor distribution provided in PDF.

rows.Risk Transfer Flow: Investors → Fusion notes → fully collateralized retrocession protection purchased by Swiss Re
rows.Notes on Structure: Provides Swiss Re retrocession for typhoon losses in Japan and earthquake losses in Mexico (linked to FONDEN).
